saida_bruta:
“Your face looks familiar,” he remarked courteously. “Weren’t you with the First Division in the war?”

“Why yes. I served in the Twenty-eighth Infantry.”

“I was with the Sixteenth till June nineteen-eighteen. I knew I’d met you somewhere before.”

We chatted briefly about some damp, grey little villages in France. Apparently he resided nearby, since he mentioned he’d just purchased a hydroplane and planned to test it in the morning.

“Care to join me, old sport? Just close to the shore along the Sound.”

“What time?”

“Whenever suits you best.”

I nearly asked his name when Jordan glanced over and smiled.

“Having a good time now?” she asked.

“Much better.” I faced my new acquaintance again. “This is a strange party for me. I haven’t even met the host. I live over there—” I gestured toward the unseen hedge afar, “and this fellow Gatsby sent his chauffeur with an invitation.”

For an instant he stared as though he didn’t comprehend.

“I’m Gatsby,” he declared abruptly.

“What!” I cried. “Oh, I beg your pardon.”

“I thought you knew, old sport. I’m afraid I’m not a very good host.”

saida_bruta:
Your face seems familiar,” he observed politely. “Weren’t you in the First Division during the war?”

“Why yes. I was in the Twenty-eighth Infantry.”

“I served with the Sixteenth until June nineteen-eighteen. I felt sure I’d seen you somewhere earlier.”

We talked shortly of several damp, grey small villages in France. Clearly he lived close by, as he said he’d recently bought a hydroplane and meant to try it out at dawn.

“Want to come along, old sport? Simply near the coast by the Sound.”

“What hour?”

“Any time that works for you.”

I almost inquired his name when Jordan looked across and grinned.

“Enjoying yourself yet?” she inquired.

“Far better.” I turned to my fresh companion once more. “This is an odd gathering for me. I don’t even know the host. I dwell over there—” I pointed to the hidden hedge distant, “and this chap Gatsby had his driver bring an invite.”

For a moment he gazed as if he failed to grasp it.

“I’m Gatsby,” he stated suddenly.

“What!” I exclaimed. “Oh, please excuse me.”

“I assumed you realized, old sport. I fear I’m not an especially fine host.

saida_filtrada:
Your face seems familiar,” he remarked politely. “Weren’t you with the First Division during the war?”

“Yes, of course. I was in the Twenty-eighth Infantry.”

“I remained with the Sixteenth through June nineteen-eighteen. I felt sure I’d encountered you earlier.”

We talked awhile of various damp, gray little towns in France. Evidently he lived close by, since he said he’d recently bought a hydroplane and planned to try it at dawn.

“Want to come along, old sport? Right off the beach by the Sound.”

“What hour?”

“Any time that suits you.”

I almost inquired his name when Jordan looked across and smiled.

“Enjoying yourself finally?” she asked.

“Far better.” I turned back to my new acquaintance. “This is an odd party for me. I’ve yet to meet the host. I live over there—” I pointed to the far hidden hedge, “and this fellow Gatsby dispatched his chauffeur with an invite.”

He gazed a moment as if confused.

“I’m Gatsby,” he said suddenly.

“What!” I exclaimed. “Oh, please excuse me.”

“I thought you realized, old sport. I guess I’m not an especially fine host.

saida_bruta:
Your face looks familiar,” he observed courteously. “Weren’t you in the First Division in the war?”

“Yes, indeed. I served in the Twenty-eighth Infantry.”

“I stayed with the Sixteenth until June nineteen-eighteen. I was certain I’d met you before.”

We chatted a bit about assorted damp, gray small towns in France. Clearly he resided nearby, for he mentioned he’d lately purchased a hydroplane and meant to test it at daybreak.

“Care to join me, old sport? Straight from the beach near the Sound.”

“What time?”

“Whenever works for you.”

I nearly asked his name before Jordan glanced over and grinned.

“Having fun at last?” she inquired.

“Much more.” I faced my new friend again. “This is a strange gathering for me. I still haven’t met the host. I reside over there—” I indicated the distant concealed hedge, “and this chap Gatsby sent his driver with an invitation.”

He stared briefly as though puzzled.

“I’m Gatsby,” he declared abruptly.

“What!” I cried. “Oh, do forgive me.”

“I assumed you knew, old sport. I suppose I’m not a particularly good host.
